name: pier
description: Automated static website deployment project with GitHub Actions CI/CD to Azure VPS, supports HTTPS/Nginx/Docker/LetsEncrypt

files.docs/architecture.md: High-level architecture overview: system context, user flow, container boundaries, security perimeter
files.docs/requirements.md: Project requirements: features, legal disclaimers, access rules, priorities
files.docs/tech-design.md: Detailed technical design: TypeScript + Express + PostgreSQL, Docker Compose, SendGrid, route design, file storage
files.src/index.html: Old static homepage (will be replaced by Node.js app)
files.Dockerfile: FROM nginx:alpine, copies src/index.html, nginx.conf, entrypoint.sh, exposes 80+443, runs entrypoint.sh
files.nginx.conf: Fallback HTTP-only config for initial startup before Let's Encrypt certs exist; the real HTTPS config is generated by entrypoint.sh
files.entrypoint.sh: Container entrypoint: if /etc/letsencrypt/live/ailaopo.online/fullchain.pem exists, rewrite nginx.conf with HTTPS + HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect, then start nginx
files.docker-compose.yml: Outdated - kept for reference but NOT used in production (GitHub Actions handles deployment)
files..github/workflows/deploy.yml: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline (see 'workflow' section below)

secrets_required.DOCKER_PASSWORD: Docker Hub Access Token with repo write permission

steps: Login to Docker Hub using secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME + secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD
Build and push image with 2 tags: :latest and :$VERSION
docker pull pier:$VERSION (uses version tag, not :latest)
Verify: sleep 5, check docker inspect State.Running

workflow.confirm_working_directory: When SSH executes commands, the working directory is the default SSH working directory (usually ~). All paths in scripts must be absolute.

architecture.description: Git push -> GitHub Actions builds Docker image -> Push to Docker Hub -> SSH to Azure VPS -> Pull image -> Run container
architecture.cert_flow: Let's Encrypt certs stored on VPS host at /etc/letsencrypt, mounted to container at /etc/letsencrypt. Entrypoint.sh detects cert existence and generates appropriate nginx config
architecture.auto_restart: --restart=always ensures container starts after VPS reboot (VPS reboots daily at 3AM UTC+8)
architecture.image_versioning: Each build pushes :latest + a version tag YYYYMMDD-XXXXXXXX. The VPS runs the specific version tag for traceability

security_notes.warning: DOCKER_PASSWORD is echoed via stdin in appleboy SSH script (line 58). GitHub masks secrets in logs, but consider using a read-only Docker Hub token for pull-only operations to minimize risk
security_notes.ssh_key: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY is passed securely via appleboy/ssh-action parameter, never written to script
security_notes.letsencrypt: Certificates stored on host, mounted read-only into container
security_notes.secrets: All secrets accessed via ${{ secrets.X }} - never hardcoded in repo
security_notes.sensitive_files: Never commit .env, credentials.json, or private keys to repo

troubleshooting.container_exits_immediately: Check docker logs pier. Common: missing SSL certs (run entrypoint.sh logic correctly). Fix: ensure /etc/letsencrypt/live/ailaopo.online/ exists on host
troubleshooting.cant_pull_image: Docker login failed or image is private. Ensure DOCKER_USERNAME + DOCKER_PASSWORD secrets are correct
troubleshooting.certbot_fails: Domain must point to VPS IP. Port 80 must be accessible. Use: docker run temp nginx, then certbot --webroot
troubleshooting.cert_not_found_in_container: Verify -v /etc/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt mount is correct. Certs stay on host, not in a docker volume
troubleshooting.github_actions_fails: Check Actions tab for error logs. Common: missing secrets, SSH key not added to VPS authorized_keys, SSH_HOST wrong
troubleshooting.version_not_applied: Ensure workflow uses needs.build-and-push.outputs.version, not hardcoded :latest
troubleshooting.vps_reboot: Container auto-starts via --restart=always. If not, run: docker update --restart=always pier

ai_usage_guidelines.language: Use English for all code comments, commit messages, config files, and documentation
workflow: 1. Before any analysis or modification, READ this opencode.json file first
2. Understand the full architecture before making changes
3. After modifying src/index.html, commit and push to trigger deployment
4. After modifying .github/workflows/deploy.yml, commit and push to update pipeline
5. Always use git add, commit, push from the repo root
ai_usage_guidelines.commit_message_format: <action>: <brief description> (e.g. 'Fix: update nginx config for cert path')
do_not: Use Chinese in commit messages or code comments
Hardcode domain names that should be configurable
ai_usage_guidelines.local_environment: Git is at C:\Program Files\Git\cmd\git.exe. Use '& "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd\git.exe" -C "C:\repositories\pier" <command>'
ai_usage_guidelines.proxy: Sing-box proxy runs at 127.0.0.1:7890 (auth: mo:shenqi123!). GitHub is whitelisted in config.json to go direct. If proxy is off, remove git proxy config before push
